flashcards.Back: Administrative access to a device using a text-based interface. on APs and WLCs, this is typically done via Console, Telnet, or SSH.
Administrative access using a web browser. most WLC configuration is performed via a web-based graphical interface using HTTP or HTTPS.
A legacy management protocol that sends data in clear text. it is highly insecure and should be disabled on APs and WLCs in favor of SSH.
A cryptographic protocol for secure CLI access. it encrypts the entire session, protecting administrative credentials and configuration data.
The unencrypted web protocol for GUI access. like Telnet, it is insecure and should be replaced by HTTPS for management tasks.
A secure version of HTTP that uses SSL/TLS to encrypt communication between the administrator's browser and the WLC's management interface.
The specific IP assigned to an AP or WLC's management interface that administrators use to establish remote connections.
The process of verifying management user credentials against a database stored locally on the AP or Wireless LAN Controller hardware.
The practice of using a centralized server (like Cisco ISE) to manage administrative login credentials for all networking hardware.
Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus. A Cisco-proprietary AAA protocol that encrypts the entire packet and separates authentication, authorization, and accounting.
Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service. An open-standard AAA protocol that combines authentication and authorization and only encrypts the password field.
The first step in AAA that verifies 'who you are' by checking a username and password against a database.
The second step in AAA that determines 'what you can do', such as defining the privilege level or specific commands an administrator is allowed to run.
The final step in AAA that records 'what you did', creating an audit trail of all commands executed and configuration changes made during a session.
A physical out-of-band management method that provides CLI access to an AP or WLC via a serial cable even if the network is down.
The logical ports used for remote CLI connections (Telnet/SSH) to a Cisco device. APs and WLCs have multiple VTY lines to support concurrent users.
A setting that defines the degree of access an administrator has. Level 15 provides full unrestricted control, while lower levels provide limited read-only or operator access.
A management account type that can monitor the status and configuration of a WLC but cannot make any changes or execute disruptive commands.
A management account type with full administrative authority to modify any configuration parameter on the Wireless LAN Controller.
A security feature that automatically disconnects an inactive management session after a pre-defined period to prevent unauthorized access from abandoned terminals.
A protocol used by network monitoring systems to retrieve status information and performance metrics from APs and WLCs.
A service that sends system messages and alerts from the WLC to a central server for logging and long-term analysis.
A digital file used by the WLC to prove its identity during HTTPS or SSH sessions, ensuring the administrator is connecting to the correct device.
A specialized WLC user role that can only create and manage temporary guest user accounts, typically used by non-technical reception staff.
The address used by a lightweight AP to communicate with its WLC. it is separate from the IP addresses used by wireless clients.
Accessing the WLC via its physical service port, which is restricted to a local management VLAN for increased security.
The practice of strictly using HTTPS for all browser-based interaction with the Wireless LAN Controller GUI.
An ACL applied to the VTY lines to restrict remote management access to specific trusted management workstations or subnets.
An organizational requirement for management passwords, such as minimum length, complexity, and mandatory periodic changes.
A feature of TACACS+ that records every single command entered by an administrator, providing a detailed audit log for security compliance.
